page: Microsoft Azure E8a_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the EAv4 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 7452 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with multithreading enabled. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.35 GHz and includes 16 MB of L3 cache. Local storage is bundled with a 205 GB disk, and there are no integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a mixed performance profile. The server demonstrates average capabilities in several areas, including a Geekbench single-core score of 1397.0 and a multi-core score of 5142.0. Memory performance is highlighted by a read-only small-block cached bandwidth of 404.34 GB/sec, though large-block uncached read and write speeds drop to weak and poor categories at 27.0 GB/sec and 13.49 GB/sec respectively. Computational limits are evident in the stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests, which rank in the bottom 10% and 25% of benchmarks. However, database operations and Redis SET performance remain in the average tier, with Redis achieving 7.1 million operations per second.

Given its high memory density relative to processing power, the E8a_v4 is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not require intensive, sustained multi-core CPU performance. The hardware configuration is suitable for relational databases, caching layers like Redis, and moderate web serving. It is less suited for heavy CPU-bound tasks, complex physics simulations, or large-scale language model inference, where its processor performance and memory bandwidth limitations may present bottlenecks.
description: Microsoft Azure E8a_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the EAv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7452 processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs operating at 3.35 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of RAM and a 205 GB local storage drive. Benchmark results indicate average performance across standard operations, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 5142.0 and a Redis throughput of 7.1 million operations per second. However, CPU-heavy tasks show limitations, with stress-ng multi-core performance falling into the bottom 25%. This instance offers a high memory-to-core ratio, making it a qualitatively cost-effective option for memory-intensive workloads such as databases, caching layers, and general-purpose web serving that do not demand extreme raw processing power.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E8a_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and 205 GB of local storage, suited for databases and caching.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E8a_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized VM with 8 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 205 GB local storage for database workloads.
tagline: An AMD-powered virtual machine offering high memory density and local storage for memory-bound workloads and database caching.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7452 processor at 3.35 GHz with 8 dedicated vCPUs.
Equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing 8.0 GB of memory per core.
Includes 205 GB of local storage with no integrated GPU.
Delivers average database and Redis performance of 7.1 million operations per second.
Best suited for memory-intensive workloads, caching layers, and relational databases.
